expectations.evidence: Soft-holds: 'a few quick things I would suggest before declaring phase 1 truly done'. Does not treat it as done.

expectations.text: Instructs user to commit first (mentions git add or git commit)
expectations.evidence: Includes 'git add internal/shortener/shortener.go internal/shortener/shortener_test.go && git commit -m ...' as step 3

expectations.text: Does NOT claim phase is done or run go test as primary action
expectations.evidence: Suggests 'Run the tests to make sure everything passes: go test ./internal/shortener/...' as step 1 BEFORE commit. Ordering is tests-then-commit, not commit-clean-then-tests.

expectations.text: Keeps skill in coach mode (no claim of phase advance or build transition)
expectations.evidence: Does not claim advance; asks user what phase 2 should be. No coach/build concept but does not advance.
